rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY VS. CURTIS KEKOA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defts To Serve Further Responses And Responses To Documents Production Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 19, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY'S Motion to Compel Defendants to Serve Further Responses and Responses to Documents Production Requests for Production of Documents and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LONG C NGUYEN et al VS. RICHARD WOOSTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 19, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LONG NGUYEN's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Hearing required. Counsel did not timely file proof of service of the motion with the Court. "Proof of service of the moving papers must be filed no later than five court days before the time appointed for the hearing." (CRC 3.1300(c).) Counsel must bring proof of service of the motion to the Court by 9:15 a.m. on the morning of the hearing or the motion will be taken off calendar. If possible counsel is also requested to send the proof of service electronically to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Further, counsel must bring a proposed form of Order to the hearing on using the mandatory Judicial Council form. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: VALERYA SEROVAYSKAYA VS. LILYA VAISMAN DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 19, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT KRISTIAN LUNDGREN-KOSZEGHY, DMD,MMSC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Defendant is not entitled to summary judgment. As to the first cause of action for professional negligence, the cause of action is not barred by the statute of limitations per CCP § 340.6. The operative date for the running of the one year statute of limitations was the discovery of her injury on April 20, 2011. (See Points & Auth., p. 7:18-20; Opp., 6:25-26.) Plaintiff served her notice of intention to commence the action on March 30, 2012, and the time for commencement of the action was extended by 90 days to June 30, 2012. (CCP § 364(d). See Serovayskaya Decl., 27, Exhibit H.) Plaintiff timely filed her Complaint on June 28, 2012. (UMF no.1.) As to whether Defendant's conduct fell within the community standard of care, Plaintiff has come forward with conflicting expert evidence in the declaration of Don Missirlian, D.D.S., and Defendant is not entitled to summary judgment. (Munro v. Regents of University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 984-85.) As to the second cause of action for concealment, Defendant's argument is predicated on prevailing on the first cause of action, and the motion for summary judgment is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN L. COCKHREN II VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Harjindar Chima, Chima, Harjindar S., Trustee, 1996 Harjindar And Margaret Chima Family Trust'S Motion To Transfer Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 19, 2013, Line 14, Defendants Harjindar Chima, Chima, Harjindar S., Trustee, 1996 Harjindar And Margaret Chima Family Trust'S Motion To Transfer Action. Denied, without prejudice. Defendants have not sufficiently demonstrated that the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice will be promoted by transfer to Solano County. (CCP § 397(d).) At the time of filing of the motion, no discovery had commenced, and Defendants' motion is based on the anticipation that they will call a number of witnesses in Solano County. (Dashman Boer Decl., 6.) Evidence proffered in reply is improper and will not be considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
